---
author: Tuomas Koskela
date: 2026-07-06
format: revealjs
institute: "[UCL ARC](https://www.ucl.ac.uk/advanced-research-computing)"
subtitle: "[ICS 2026 Workshop on Reproducible and Sustainable HPC Benchmarking](https://software.llnl.gov/benchpark/ics.html)"
title: "Reflections on Building Portable Benchmarking Frameworks"
---

# Who am I?

## Who am I? {data-menu-title="Who am I? (i)"}

- I am a research software engineer at UCL
- I lead a team of HPC Research Software Engineers at UCL
- We mostly work on HPC research applications and tools
- I don’t run a HPC system
- I don’t own the applications I work on

# Motivation

## Who Wouldn't Want Automatic Benchmarks

- Sysadmins
- Developers
- Researchers
- Procurement’ers

## It is not that hard

- Wrapping around build systems
- Templating for scheduler scripts
- Output parsing
- Logging

## Disclosure: I did this too

- Excalibur-tests project
  - Clear separation of platforms and benchmarks
  - Support everything we can get our hands on
  - Engage with local teams for maintenance
- Users
  - 1 team of RSE’s (mine)
  - 1 postdoc (Co-I’s)
  - 1 team of sysadmins (maybe)
